feed.title: Mayur Sinha
feed.subtitle: Principal Security Architect building sovereign control infrastructure for AI agents and adversarial systems.

entry.title: Three Trust Paths for Governing AI Agent Architectures
entry.summary: Agentic architectures surface three trust paths — data movement, transaction integrity, and tool execution — that share a security model but require different controls, invariants, and failure behavior.

entry.title: MCP Visor: Runtime Policy Enforcement
entry.summary: MCP Visor turns MCP tool execution into a deterministic policy boundary: inspect the tool call, enforce the rule, redact secrets, require approval, and log the decision before the action reaches the server.

entry.title: Why grinding is a self-inflicted DDoS
entry.summary: Grinding inside an extractive system does not change the payoff matrix. It only increases throughput for someone else's infrastructure.

entry.title: How to eliminate an entire class of API authorization bugs
entry.summary: BOLA is not solved by asking every developer to remember object-level checks. It is solved by making authorization a reusable platform capability.

entry.title: MCP Security Is a Supply Chain Risk
entry.summary: MCP servers are not harmless plugins. They are executable trust boundaries that shape what AI agents see, believe, and do.

entry.title: Epistemic Security for AI Agents
entry.summary: AI agents do not only need runtime security. They need epistemic security: controls for provenance, uncertainty, evidence, memory, and human verification.

entry.title: The AI Agent Dilemma
entry.summary: AI agents may not just change how we work. They may weaken our ability to know, verify, and justify what we believe.

entry.title: Game theory for cybersecurity: from reactive defense to strategic resilience
entry.summary: A practical guide to using game theory in cybersecurity, from Nash and Stackelberg models to APT defense, forensic readiness, and LLM-driven simulations.

entry.title: Threat Modeling Autonomous AI Agents in Production
entry.summary: A practical framework for threat modeling autonomous AI agents in production, with concrete attack paths, controls, and measurable security SLOs.

entry.title: Traditional manual code review is no longer sustainable
entry.summary: Code review was built for a world where humans wrote most code. In the AI era, the bottleneck moves from verifying syntax to governing intent.

entry.title: Why Transformer LLMs are better at finding code vulnerabilities than classical neural networks
entry.summary: A technical comparison of classical ANN pipelines and transformer LLMs for vulnerability detection.

entry.title: Vibe Engineering
entry.summary: Vibe coding ships fast and breaks things. Vibe engineering keeps the speed but adds the discipline that makes software survive contact with production.

entry.title: Designing a living ecosystem of AI agents
entry.summary: The next step past agent orchestration is not bigger orchestrators. It is ecosystems where agents spawn, bid, learn, and retire.

entry.title: Life is cruel
entry.summary: The universe is indifferent to suffering. The only justice available is the justice we build.

entry.title: Revisiting the Unabomber
entry.summary: Kaczynski's manifesto identified real problems with industrial society. His solution was murder. The honest move is to separate the diagnosis from the cure.

entry.title: Nietzsche and Vedanta: Unexpected Parallels
entry.summary: Nietzsche and Vedanta agree that conventional categories are provisional. They diverge on what to do once those categories collapse.

entry.title: The Epicurean Paradox from Advaita Vedanta
entry.summary: Epicurus turns the existence of evil into a logical trap for theism. Advaita Vedanta escapes the trap by denying the duality the trap depends on.

entry.title: Elon Musk on xAI
entry.summary: xAI's mission is to build a truth-seeking AGI. The part that stayed with me was not the ambition, but the first-principles habit behind it.

entry.title: Man is a machine
entry.summary: We are biological machines and first-person subjects at the same time. The mistake is treating one description as the whole story.

entry.title: Was Thanos Right?
entry.summary: Thanos asks a real question about scarcity and survival, but his answer reveals the danger of treating human lives as variables in a single equation.
